title: rubus silvaticus subsp. leyi
intro: Rubus silvaticus subsp. leyi, also known as the wild bramble or woodland bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus silvaticus plant, a type of bramble or blackberry native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus silvaticus subsp. leyi include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Rubus silvaticus subsp. leyi can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
